title: Praxis
description: Execution layer of the four-system cognitive stack (Mnemos, Chronos, Nous, Praxis). Exposes a registry of capabilities and executes actions under policy with idempotency, dry-run, and audit. Praxis does not decide; that belongs to Nous. This spec covers Phase 1 (MVP), Phase 2 (Policy & Resilience) and Phase 3 (Capability Ecosystem) per Roadmap.md.

features.title: Foundation Scaffolding
features.description: Repo scaffolding to make the documented patterns runnable. Go module pinned to Mnemos foundation library versions (bolt, fortify, mcp-go, statekit, axi-go), Makefile mirroring Mnemos targets, sqlc.yaml for sqlite + postgres engines, .gitignore, .editorconfig, .golangci.yml, MIT LICENSE.

features.title: Domain and Storage Foundation
features.description: DDD domain types (Capability, Action, Result, Simulation, PolicyDecision, CallerRef, ActionError, AuditEvent), statekit-backed Action lifecycle FSM, repository ports, and three storage backends (memory as canonical reference, sqlite local-first, postgres production) behind a shared store contract test suite. Backend selection via PRAXIS_DB_TYPE / PRAXIS_DB_CONN.

features.title: Capability Registry and Schema Validation
features.description: In-process CapabilityRegistry with programmatic Register(handler), and a schema validator gating every Execute and DryRun. Validates Action.Payload against InputSchema before any side effect and handler Output against OutputSchema after execution. Supports JSON Schema and Go-struct registration.

features.title: Executor (Execute and DryRun)
features.description: Linear executor flow per TDD section 5 - resolve capability, validate schema, evaluate policy, check idempotency, run handler with timeout and panic recovery, persist result and audit, emit outcome. Phase-1 PolicyEngine is a global allow-list with explainable PolicyDecision recorded on every action. Status transitions go through the statekit FSM.

features.title: Audit Log
features.description: Append-only record of every action lifecycle event (received, validated, policy, executed, succeeded, failed, simulated). Search by capability, caller, time window, action ID. Replay-from-audit canary test reconstructs an action's full lifecycle from audit_events alone and must always pass.

features.title: Real Capability Handlers
features.description: Two production-quality handlers proving the abstraction. send_message via Slack Web API forwarding client_msg_id for idempotency. send_email via SMTP with deterministic Message-ID. Vendor-sandboxed integration tests skipped without credentials. Errors classified as retryable vs non-retryable.

features.title: Outcome Writeback to Mnemos
features.description: After every terminal action, post a praxis.action_completed event to Mnemos so the cognitive loop closes. fortify/retry with Mnemos-equivalent defaults (5xx + 429 retry, 4xx fail fast, exponential backoff with jitter). Outbox-and-retry on Mnemos unavailability; never block Execute. Configurable via PRAXIS_MNEMOS_URL / PRAXIS_MNEMOS_TOKEN.

features.title: HTTP API and CLI
features.description: Public surface for callers - axi-go HTTP server (cmd/praxis/axikernel.go mirroring Mnemos's axikernel pattern) and CLI wrapping the same executor. Endpoints GET /health, GET /v1/capabilities, GET /v1/capabilities/{name}, POST /v1/actions, GET /v1/actions/{id}, POST /v1/actions/{id}/dry-run, GET /v1/audit. CLI commands praxis caps list/show, praxis run, praxis log show, praxis serve. Structured logs via bolt; basic Prometheus metrics.

features.title: Public Go Client
features.description: Typed Go client for callers, mirroring Mnemos client patterns. New constructor accepting WithToken, WithLogger (bolt), WithRetry (fortify). Methods ListCapabilities, Execute, DryRun, GetAction. APIError type with status + message, errors.As-friendly. Safe for concurrent use.

features.title: Policy Engine v2 - Default-Deny + Ordered Rules (Phase 2 M2.1)
features.description: Phase-2 PolicyEngine adds default-deny mode (controlled by PRAXIS_POLICY_MODE), explicit first-match-wins ordering with PolicyRule.Priority, and fail-closed behaviour on PolicyRepo errors. Every Execute and DryRun records the matched rule on the action.

features.title: Async Actions with At-Least-Once Drainer (Phase 2 M2.2)
features.description: Action.Mode = sync | async. Async submissions persist as validated and return 202 Accepted; jobs.Runner drains validated/async rows from the actions table and resumes through Executor.Resume. At-least-once delivery is safe via the IdempotencyKeeper. Status pollable via GET /v1/actions/{id}.

features.title: Per-Capability Retry Configuration (Phase 2 M2.3)
features.description: HandlerRunner.RunWithCapability builds a per-call fortify retry strategy from Capability.Retry (MaxAttempts, InitialDelay, MaxDelay, Multiplier), falling back to the global strategy when nil. The IsRetryable classifier is shared so semantics stay consistent across capabilities.

features.title: Per-Capability + Per-Caller Rate Limiting (Phase 2 M2.4)
features.description: fortify/ratelimit token bucket per (capability, caller) key. Capability.RateLimit declares Rate / Burst / Interval. Throttled actions emit policy_throttled audit events with retry_after_ms and are rejected with code rate_limited; the handler is never invoked.

features.title: Additional Capability Handlers (Phase 2 M2.6)
features.description: Generic http_request handler shipped (any HTTP endpoint, idempotency-key forwarding). Pending vendor handlers - GitHub create_issue and comment, Linear/Jira create_issue and transition, Calendar schedule and invite.

features.title: Vendor Retry-After Parsing (Phase 2 polish)
features.description: Parse the vendor Retry-After header (delta-seconds or HTTP-date) on 429/503 responses and surface to the retry strategy so the next attempt waits at least that long. Wire through handlerrunner so all handlers benefit without per-handler code.

features.title: Async Completion Webhook Delivery (Phase 2 polish)
features.description: Action carries an optional callback_url. After an async action reaches a terminal status, post the Result to that URL with HMAC-SHA256 signature. Reuses the outcome outbox machinery for retry on 5xx + 429.

features.title: Out-of-Tree Plugin Architecture (Phase 3 M3.1)
features.description: Stable handler ABI so third-party capability handlers can ship as separate Go modules or compiled plugins. On-disk discovery, signature verification (cosign-compatible), and a sandbox boundary enforcing CPU, memory, and network egress limits.

features.title: MCP Capability Surface (Phase 3 M3.2)
features.description: mcp-go server exposing ListCapabilities as MCP tool discovery and Execute/DryRun as tool calls. Same executor as the HTTP surface so audit + policy are uniformly applied. Capability descriptors shaped for agent-go consumption with no bespoke glue.

features.title: Multi-Tenant Policy and Audit (Phase 3 M3.3)
features.description: Org / team / user scope hierarchy for policy rules and audit retention. Per-tenant capability registries so a tenant can register private capabilities. Per-tenant audit access controls so one tenant's audit is invisible to others.

features.title: Reversibility via Capability.Compensate (Phase 3 M3.4)
features.description: Capability.Compensate(ctx, originalResult) for capabilities whose effects can be reversed. praxis revert <action-id> runs the compensating action under the same audit umbrella, linking parent and revert via audit. Best-effort flag for vendors that cannot exactly reverse.

features.title: Compliance, Audit Export, and PII Redaction (Phase 3 M3.5)
features.description: Exportable audit (JSON, CSV) suitable for SOC 2 / HIPAA / GDPR review. Configurable PII redaction policy applied at audit detail level. Org-level dashboards covering capability usage, policy denials, and error rates.

features.title: Plugin pipeline integration into cmd/praxis startup
features.description: Plugin discovery, signature verification, ABI load and sandbox wrapping are implemented as libraries but cmd/praxis bootstrap does not call them. Phase 4 wires the full pipeline: at startup, scan PRAXIS_PLUGIN_DIR, verify each Discovered against PRAXIS_PLUGIN_TRUSTED_KEYS, dlopen the artefact (Go plugin package), retrieve the exported `Plugin` symbol, call plugin.Load with the runtime registry as the Loader. Failures must (a) log the offending plugin and reason via bolt, (b) emit a metric praxis_plugin_load_total{result=...}, (c) refuse to start the server when the operator opts into strict mode (PRAXIS_PLUGIN_STRICT=1). Healthy plugins surface in /v1/capabilities. Cross-platform: Linux + macOS only (Go plugin package limitation). Replace the placeholder registerHandlers wiring with a registerCorePlus(loader) flow that registers built-in handlers first, plugins second, and rejects duplicate names.

features.title: Audit retention scheduler
features.description: Service.PurgeExpired enforces per-tenant retention windows but no background worker invokes it. Add a scheduler that runs PurgeExpired every PRAXIS_AUDIT_RETENTION_INTERVAL (default 1h), logs deletion counts per tenant, exposes praxis_audit_purge_total{org_id,result}. Scheduler shares the bootstrap context so it stops on SIGTERM; first run happens after 5 minutes of uptime to avoid blocking startup. Failure to purge for one tenant must not stop the sweep for others (per-tenant errors logged, surfaced in metric, sweep continues).

features.title: MCP and CLI tenant-aware capability discovery
features.description: HTTP /v1/capabilities derives a Caller from X-Praxis-* headers and uses ListCapabilitiesForCaller, but the MCP surface and the CLI still call the global ListCapabilities. Phase 4 plumbs caller context end-to-end: MCP server reads tenant identity from the MCP session/handshake (mcp-go conventions) and routes through ListCapabilitiesForCaller; CLI gains --org and --team flags on `praxis caps list` and `praxis caps show`. Anonymous CLI invocations stay global-only. The runtime contract is: every capability discovery path is tenant-aware or explicitly anonymous; there is no third option that silently leaks private capabilities.

features.title: Out-of-process plugin loader for true resource isolation
features.description: In-process sandbox enforces CPU timeout and HTTP egress allowlist but cannot enforce MaxMemoryBytes (Go's allocator is shared with the host process) and cannot stop a malicious plugin that bypasses plugin.HTTPClient. Phase 4 spec: each plugin runs in a child process spawned from a praxis-pluginhost binary; parent communicates via gRPC over stdin/stdout (or unix socket); host applies cgroups (Linux) / setrlimit (BSDs) for CPU and memory; network egress filtered at the host firewall layer using the AllowedHosts policy. Plugin ABI v1 stays unchanged from the plugin author's perspective: the praxis-pluginhost shim implements the Plugin interface and forwards each call across the IPC boundary. Failure modes documented: child crash → registration removed + metric incremented; OOM → MaxMemoryBytes enforced (the field gains real teeth).

features.title: Plugin lifecycle: hot reload and rotation
features.description: Today plugins are loaded once at startup and stay until process exit. Phase 4 adds: filesystem watch on PRAXIS_PLUGIN_DIR using fsnotify; on manifest.json or .sig change, re-verify and re-load the affected plugin atomically (drain in-flight calls, swap registration); SIGHUP triggers a full re-scan; graceful version rollover where the old version completes its in-flight calls while new traffic routes to the new version; CLI `praxis plugins list` shows loaded plugins with version + signature digest; `praxis plugins reload <name>` forces a single-plugin reload. Operator can opt out of file-watch via PRAXIS_PLUGIN_AUTORELOAD=0.

features.title: OpenTelemetry tracing across executor and handlers
features.description: Today every action lifecycle event hits the audit log but there is no distributed-trace propagation: a Praxis Execute called from an upstream service shows up as a black box in the caller's trace. Phase 5 adds OTel: every Execute, DryRun, Resume, and Revert opens a span; handler Execute / Simulate / Compensate inherit a child span; outbound HTTP requests via plugin.HTTPClient inject W3C traceparent so vendor latency lands in the same trace. Span attributes carry capability name, action ID, status, policy decision, and tenant OrgID. OTLP exporter wires up via PRAXIS_OTLP_ENDPOINT (gRPC) and PRAXIS_OTLP_PROTOCOL (grpc|http). Sampling configurable via PRAXIS_TRACE_SAMPLE (0..1). Existing audit log stays canonical for Praxis-internal lifecycle replay; OTel is for system-wide correlation.

features.title: cgroups v2 enforcement for out-of-process plugins (Linux)
features.description: Phase 4's setrlimit-based budget enforcement caps the plugin host process but leaks across fork/exec inside the plugin and gives no cumulative metrics. cgroups v2 closes both gaps on Linux. Phase 5 work: parent creates a per-plugin cgroup under /sys/fs/cgroup/praxis/<plugin-name>.scope; writes memory.max + cpu.max from ResourceBudget; spawns the praxis-pluginhost child via clone3 with CLONE_INTO_CGROUP so the child enters the cgroup atomically (no race window); reads memory.peak + cpu.stat at plugin reload to surface usage in /metrics. Falls back to setrlimit when cgroup mount unavailable (containers without delegated cgroups, non-systemd hosts). Required permission: the praxis user needs to own the cgroup subtree; document setup in docs/cgroups.md. macOS path stays setrlimit-only.

features.title: Capability schema versioning and compatibility checker
features.description: Today a capability's InputSchema can change between plugin reloads with no breaking-change check. A handler that accepted {to, body} could be silently replaced by one demanding {recipient, message_body} and existing callers would discover the break only at runtime. Phase 5 adds schema versioning: domain.Capability gains InputSchemaVersion + OutputSchemaVersion (semver-loose); registry rejects a re-registration whose major version dropped backward-compat (renamed required field, narrowed enum, tightened type). Compatibility check is configurable per registration: strict (refuse downgrade), warn (audit-only), off (current behaviour). Adds praxis_capability_breaking_change_total{capability,outcome} metric. Per-capability changelog rendered in /v1/capabilities/{name}.

features.title: Performance benchmark suite and SLOs
features.description: Praxis has no published latency or throughput targets. Operators have no way to tell whether their deployment is healthy beyond "tests pass." Phase 5 establishes: benchmark suite under bench/ exercising the full Execute path against the in-memory backend (synthetic handler, no vendor I/O), the SQLite backend, and the Postgres backend; targets baked into Makefile (make bench) and CI (regression detection via benchstat); published SLOs — p50 Execute latency < 2ms (memory) / < 10ms (sqlite) / < 25ms (postgres) at 100 concurrent calls; throughput floor — 5k actions/sec on memory backend. Out-of-process plugin path benchmarked separately so operators can budget the IPC cost. Bench results auto-published to docs/benchmarks.md on tag.

features.title: Federated MCP: aggregate upstream MCP servers as Praxis capabilities
features.description: Praxis sits in the cognitive stack as the execution layer; agents reach it via MCP. Phase 5 turns Praxis into an MCP federation hub so an agent talking to one Praxis instance can reach every tool the operator has approved across multiple upstream MCP servers — without each tool re-implementing policy/audit. Implementation: cmd/praxis adds an mcp.federation.yaml config listing upstream MCP servers (URL or stdio command + token); a federation goroutine connects to each upstream, fetches its tool catalogue, and registers a federatedHandler per upstream tool. Calls flow through the same executor (policy → schema → idempotency → audit → emit) before being forwarded. Audit detail records the upstream server identity. Failures (upstream down, auth rejected) deregister gracefully via the same Watchable pattern the out-of-process loader uses. Schema is fetched at federation time and surfaced unchanged — operators see one tool catalogue. Phase 5's biggest ecosystem play.

features.title: MCP federation HTTP transport
features.description: Phase 5 federation only supports stdio upstreams (mcp-go v1.9 limitation). When mcp-go ships an HTTP / SSE transport — already on its roadmap — wire URL-based upstreams in mcp.federation.yaml: HTTPS connection with Bearer token, reconnect on transport failure, surfaced via the existing Connection / Watchable contract. Drops ErrURLTransportUnsupported. Tests against a fake mcp-go HTTP server.

features.title: Sigstore Fulcio keyless signature verification for plugins
features.description: Phase 3/4 plugin signing relies on operator-managed PEM ECDSA public keys (PRAXIS_PLUGIN_TRUSTED_KEYS). Adds keyless verification via Sigstore Fulcio short-lived certs + Rekor transparency log. Operator declares trusted OIDC identities (e.g. cosign sign --identity-token ... or GitHub Actions workload identity) instead of long-lived keys. Verifier checks the cert chain against Fulcio root, looks up the artefact's Rekor entry, and validates the signed identity matches the operator's allowlist. Provides a stronger trust model — compromised long-lived key is no longer a single point of failure. Pulls in github.com/sigstore/sigstore. Falls back to the PEM bundle path when no Fulcio config is set.

features.title: mTLS on HTTP API + MCP surface
features.description: SECURITY.md lists mTLS as operator-provided (TLS-terminating proxy). Phase 6 brings it in-process: PRAXIS_TLS_CERT_FILE + PRAXIS_TLS_KEY_FILE for server TLS; PRAXIS_MTLS_CLIENT_CA_FILE upgrades to mutual TLS. Same cert/CA shared by the HTTP server and (when mcp-go grows TCP transport) the MCP server. Bearer token continues to gate /v1/* endpoints; mTLS is the orthogonal transport-level guarantee. Documented rotation procedure (zero-downtime via SIGHUP-driven cert reload).

features.title: Out-of-process plugin loader as default config flag
features.description: Phase 4/5 ship ProcessOpener + cgroup wiring but cmd/praxis bootstrap still wires DefaultOpener (in-process). Add PRAXIS_PLUGIN_OUT_OF_PROCESS=1 (or similar) that switches the loader to ProcessOpener with PRAXIS_PLUGINHOST_BINARY pointing at praxis-pluginhost. When cgroup v2 detected, also sets ProcessOpener.CgroupParent. Documents the choice trade-offs (in-process: lower latency, shared memory; out-of-process: real isolation, kernel-enforced limits).

features.title: Persistent capability changelog
features.description: Phase 5 changelog endpoint is in-memory: HistoryEntry list rebuilds from zero on every restart. Phase 6 persists it: capability_history table on sqlite + postgres + memory, migration 005, sqlc query family, RegistryHistoryRepo port. /v1/capabilities/{name}/changelog reads from disk so the audit-of-schema-drift survives process restarts and rolling deploys.

features.title: Vex.json + security.yml CI gate
features.description: SECURITY.md notes the 122 nox findings as triage-pending. Phase 6 closes that loop: per-finding OpenVEX statements in .nox/vex.json justifying each accepted finding, plus a security.yml workflow that diffs new scans against the baseline + vex (mirrors chronos's pipeline). Unbaselined findings fail CI. Operator-facing doc explaining how to add a new vex statement.
